hypothesis: Augmenting E2EFOLD’s decoder training data with a mixed-source curriculum that includes at least 30% out-of-distribution sequences designed by an external inverse-folding model (e.g., ProteinMPNN) will reduce the decoder’s average RMSD on externally designed sequences by more than 0.5 Å compared to a decoder trained only on encoder-sampled sequences, without increasing the decoder’s parameter count.
